mnemonics: S.L.O.T. - Squared Loss, Likelihood Optimization Theory: Remember that both methods (Least Squares and Maximum Likelihood) are fundamentally about optimizing an objective function to fit a time series model.
keyPoints: The sum-of-squares function measures the discrepancy between observed data and model values.
The likelihood function measures the probability of observing the data given a set of model parameters.
Under the assumption of normally distributed errors, minimizing the sum-of-squares is equivalent to maximizing the likelihood.
commonMistakes: Assuming the equivalence of Least Squares and Maximum Likelihood always holds, even when the error distribution is not normal.
Forgetting that optimization algorithms can converge to local minima/maxima instead of the global optimum for complex models.
Mis-specifying the model (e.g., wrong ARIMA order) leading to a well-fitted but meaningless objective function value.
